snippet: Schools receive funding from a variety of government and non-government sources. Most school funding is received from the department. Schools should maintain remittance advices where provided. For sources where a remittance advice is not available (e.g. parent payments, sundry debtors) schools should retain appropriate evidence to support recognition of the revenue. This may include bank statements, audit trail and source contracts/user agreements. The Student Resource Package (SRP) is the department’s main funding mechanism for Victorian government schools. It is primarily an enrolment-based funding model with a cash component being paid directly to schools in quarterly instalments. Undertake an annual reconciliation by the due date as specified in the reconciliation process for that year.
